name: Eggplant Sichuan Style
recipeIngredient: 4 tbsp Peanut or corn oil (or more if needed)
1/4 cup Chopped water chestnuts, preferably fresh
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut eggplant into 1/2-by-2-inch strips. If using Italian eggplants, peel the skin.
2. In a colander, toss eggplant with salt; drain for 30 minutes. Gently squeeze to remove excess water. Pat dry with paper towels.
3. In a small bowl, mix soy sauce, sugar and chicken stock.
4. Preheat wok until hot over high heat. Add 3 tablespoons of the oil, tilt wok to coat sides.
5. When hot, add 1 layer of eggplant, stir-fry until seared and tender (about 3 minutes). Remove to colander. Drain over a bowl to catch juices.
6. Cook remaining eggplant in same manner, adding more oil if needed.
7. Reheat wok over medium-high heat. Add remaining 1 tablespoon of the oil, ginger, garlic and chile; cook gently but do not brown.
8. Add water chestnuts and half of the green onions; stir-fry together for 5 seconds.
9. Increase to high heat, add reserved soy sauce mixture and eggplant juices; bring to a boil.
10. Return cooked eggplant; toss quickly over high heat until most of the sauce is reduced and absorbed into eggplant (about 1-to-2 minutes).
11. Fold in vinegar and sesame oil. Remove to serving dish. Top with remaining green onions and sesame seeds. Serve hot or cold.
